payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process
payload.justification: Task claiming is a strategically significant process enabling children to engage with the pocket money system.

payload.description: Task definition and compensation assignment process
payload.justification: The process of defining tasks and assigning monetary values is strategically critical for parental control.

payload.description: Earnings tracking and reporting process
payload.justification: Earnings tracking is a strategically important process supporting both child visibility and parental payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Performance reporting and accountability process
payload.justification: Performance and history reporting represent strategic processes enabling oversight and trend analysis.

payload.description: Child task and earnings management system (task assignment, completion history, and earnings record)
payload.justification: Task assignments are strategically significant entities central to the pocket money system, referenced across multiple signals. | Earnings represent a strategically important entity tracked for both child visibility and parental payment fulfillment. | Historical task records are strategically important entities supporting accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task
payload.justification: Monetary values are strategically significant attributes of task assignments, enabling compensation calculation.

payload.description: Parent-child task-based compensation system where parent assigns compensated tasks and child claims and completes them to earn payment
payload.justification: The assignment relationship between parent and child through tasks is strategically fundamental to the enterprise model. | The claiming and completion relationship between child and task defines the core strategic value exchange.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money task claiming, tracking, and earnings summary reporting cycle
payload.justification: Signals reference weekly periodicity for task claiming and completion tracking, indicating a strategic time cycle. | Signals indicate weekly earnings visibility, suggesting a recurring strategic cycle for financial reporting.

payload.description: Child user claiming and tracking pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as strategic actors performing task claiming, tracking, and earnings viewing activities.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and tracking child performance
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify parents as strategic actors responsible for task definition, performance monitoring, and payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Enable children to view, claim, and track available and completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Multiple signals articulate the fundamental strategic purpose of enabling task claiming and tracking for children, clearly expressing an enterprise goal. | Signal captures the strategic purpose of transparency for child users regarding task status and availability.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signals express parental motivation to establish chore-based incentive structures, representing a strategic goal for parental stakeholders.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal establishes the strategic need for performance tracking to support payment fulfillment, a clear parental goal.

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal indicates stakeholder requirement for historical data analysis, representing a strategic goal supporting accountability.

payload.description: Enable tracking of child earnings for payment fulfillment and visibility
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify the motivational goal of providing earnings visibility to children on a weekly cycle. | Signals express the parent-oriented purpose of monitoring earnings to facilitate timely and accurate payment.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Work Stewardship
payload.description: The enterprise's commitment to defining, assigning, and governing compensated responsibilities within the household, establishing what constitutes valued contribution and how it is recognized through monetary association.

payload.name: Participant Engagement
payload.description: The enterprise's obligation to enable household members to understand available opportunities, express commitment through claiming, and maintain visibility into their own contributions and outcomes.

payload.name: Economic Accountability
payload.description: The enterprise's responsibility to maintain transparent records of earned compensation, ensure accurate payment fulfillment, and provide authoritative reporting on financial obligations owed to participants.

payload.name: Performance Oversight
payload.description: The enterprise's commitment to provide longitudinal transparency into contribution patterns, enable evaluative insight for governance decisions, and support accountability through historical trend visibility.

payload.name: Intergenerational Relationship Governance
payload.description: The enterprise's foundational commitment to structure and sustain the parent-child compensatory relationship, defining the terms under which authority is exercised, obligations are created, and reciprocal accountability exists.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of tasks, task completion history, and earnings.
payload.rationale: Entity CCI describes the structural composition of task and earnings records that the enterprise is accountable for maintaining at scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall associate monetary values with tasks.
payload.rationale: Attribute CCI establishes the structural relationship between tasks and their monetary values, a fundamental enterprise commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process for defining tasks and assigning compensation.
payload.rationale: Process CCI describes the enterprise capability to define tasks and assign their associated compensation, a core functional obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks with assigned monetary values.
payload.rationale: Actor and Goal CCIs converge on a single obligation: enabling parents to define compensated task assignments. The Who identifies the stakeholder role; the Why expresses the commitment to enable their task-definition capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Why-column goal to enable children to view available tasks; expressed as an enterprise capability commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the How-column claiming process, Who-column child actor claiming capability, and Why-column claiming goal into a single enterprise commitment to enable the claiming action.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Why-column goal to enable tracking of completed tasks; expressed as an enterprise capability to provide visibility into completion state.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view their earnings.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Who-column tracking capability and Why-column earnings tracking goal; expressed as an enterprise commitment to enable visibility into accumulated earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of child earnings for payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Why-column goal to track earnings for payment fulfillment; expressed as an enterprise accountability obligation to maintain earnings data.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of tasks assigned to children, task completion history, and earnings accrued.
payload.rationale: Entity CCI describing the structural composition of the task and earnings management domain — the enterprise is accountable for maintaining these core records.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a monetary value for each task.
payload.rationale: Attribute CCI asserting that tasks carry a monetary value attribute — a structural property the enterprise must establish.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide a process for tracking and reporting earnings.
payload.rationale: How-column process CCI — the enterprise commits to providing the capability to track and report earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks, track their completion, and receive earnings summaries on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle CCI combined with Why-column goal CCI expressing a unified obligation: the enterprise enables children to claim, track, and receive summaries within a weekly cycle to support payment fulfillment and visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a record of task assignments, task completion history, and child earnings.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to maintain the foundational entities (task, completion history, earnings) that support the compensated task system. The What-column Entity CCI identifies the structural composition of the domain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a parent-child task-based compensation relationship in which parents assign compensated tasks and children claim and complete tasks to earn payment.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between parent, child, and task entities that forms the enterprise's accountability model. The What-column Relationship CCI identifies the key associations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide performance reporting and accountability to support governance decisions.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's obligation to deliver evaluative insight through performance reporting, derived from the How-column Process CCI. The function is an enterprise-level capability commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings summary reporting.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to a weekly reporting cycle for task and earnings summaries. The When-column Cycle CCI establishes the temporal obligation.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings summary reports are generated at weekly intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to access child performance reports for compensation distribution decisions.
payload.rationale: Obligates the enterprise to provide parents with access to performance data that informs accurate compensation. The Why-column Goal CCI expresses the motivational commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to maintaining and exposing historical task data to enable trend visibility and accountability over time. The Why-column Goal CCI establishes this transparency obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a parent-child task-based compensation system.
payload.rationale: Defines the fundamental structure of the compensatory relationship - a system that connects parents, children, tasks, and compensation as related entities at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to assign compensated tasks.
payload.rationale: Parent authority to create and assign tasks is a core functional commitment derived from the What-relationship and Who-actor CCIs.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim and complete tasks to earn payment.
payload.rationale: Child capability to claim, complete, and earn payment is the reciprocal functional commitment in the compensatory relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to track child performance.
payload.rationale: Parent oversight capability is a distinct functional obligation supporting accountability in the relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to track pocket money tasks.
payload.rationale: Child visibility into their own task portfolio is a distinct functional obligation supporting their participation in the compensatory relationship.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
Articulates the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Expresses the motivational goal of parents defining chore-based monetary incentives for children.
Conveys the motivational goal of providing children visibility into weekly earnings achievement.
Expresses the parent-oriented purpose of tracking child earnings to facilitate payment fulfillment.

Conveys the motivational goal of providing children visibility into weekly earnings achievement.
Expresses the parent-oriented purpose of tracking child earnings to facilitate payment fulfillment.
